citation: Woffinden, Eckman, and Robinson, Optimized Trajectory Correction Burn Placement for the NASA Artemis II Mission
mission_and_task: Artemis II robust correction-burn placement
dynamics_fidelity: Integrated cislunar GN&C linear covariance model with maneuver, navigation, venting, and crew-timeline effects
objective_and_constraints: Genetic optimization of nominal plus 3-sigma OTC2-RTC3 delta-v; EI ellipse, lunar altitude, burn timing, and optional crew-sleep penalties
uncertainty_treatment: Linear covariance propagation of environment, navigation, measurement, process, and actuator uncertainty
input_data_and_availability: Public paper exposes selected assumptions and bounds; mission-owned implementation and full covariance are unavailable
classical_algorithm: Linear covariance analysis embedded in a genetic algorithm
quantum_resources: not_applicable
optimizer_and_tuning: GA population 60 and 15 generations; authors state runtime motivated the compromise
split_and_leakage_controls: not_applicable; not a learned surrogate study
classical_baselines: Notional burn schedule and three optimization formulations
primary_metrics: Robust total correction delta-v and entry-interface dispersion margins
uncertainty_and_statistics: Covariance-based 3-sigma analysis; no public flight covariance or distributional validation
failure_reporting: Constraint penalties and runtime limitations described
reported_limitations: Notional preflight Artemis II analysis; mission-owned details are unavailable
gate4_implication: Defines the primary target, hard constraints, uncertainty variables, and strong numerical reference

citation: De Grossi et al., Transcription and Optimization of an Interplanetary Trajectory through Quantum Annealing
mission_and_task: Earth-Mars low-thrust trajectory transcription and optimization
objective_and_constraints: Binary transcription of dynamics and trajectory objective into QUBO form
classical_algorithm: Linearization, pseudospectral transcription, and hybrid classical-quantum solvers
quantum_algorithm: D-Wave quantum annealing; this is optimization, not QML
quantum_resources: Fully quantum and hybrid annealers; implementable dimensions and hardware limits discussed
optimizer_and_tuning: Binary representation and penalty construction are central design choices
classical_baselines: Hybrid and fully quantum solver comparisons; no replacement for supervised QML baselines
failure_reporting: Current hardware dimensional limits explicitly discussed
reported_limitations: Earth-Mars case; annealing result cannot establish QML or cislunar advantage
gate4_implication: Keep annealing/QAOA outside the registered QML benchmark

citation: Bowles, Ahmed, and Schuld, Better than Classical? The Subtle Art of Benchmarking Quantum Machine Learning Models
mission_and_task: Fair comparison of 12 QML models on 160 classical-data classification datasets
objective_and_constraints: Classification benchmark with controlled experimental design
uncertainty_treatment: Multiple datasets and architectures; no spacecraft uncertainty
input_data_and_availability: Six tasks generating 160 datasets; open benchmark package
quantum_algorithm: Popular variational and kernel QML classifiers
quantum_resources: Classical quantum simulation at small scales; entanglement ablations
optimizer_and_tuning: Systematic model study with architecture comparisons
split_and_leakage_controls: Registered benchmark splits in open package
failure_reporting: Reports classical wins and cases where removing entanglement does not hurt
reported_limitations: Small synthetic/classical tasks and simulation do not establish practical quantum advantage
gate4_implication: Require unrestricted classical baselines, entanglement ablation, and negative-result reporting

citation: Skolik, Jerbi, and Dunjko, Quantum Agents in the Gym: A Variational Quantum Algorithm for Deep Q-Learning
mission_and_task: Deep Q-learning on discrete and continuous-state Gym tasks
dynamics_fidelity: Generic control environments, not spacecraft dynamics
objective_and_constraints: Q-value learning under benchmark task rewards
input_data_and_availability: Benchmark environments and public paper/code context
quantum_resources: Encoding, observables, PQC architecture, and simulation conditions reported
optimizer_and_tuning: Extensive PQC and neural-network hyperparameter search with parameter comparisons
split_and_leakage_controls: Environment evaluation; not a held-out regression split
classical_baselines: Classical DQN with varying parameter counts
uncertainty_and_statistics: Architecture choices dominate parameter count in reported tasks
failure_reporting: Ablates encoding/readout and discusses restricted separation claims
reported_limitations: Simple Gym tasks; no spacecraft or hardware advantage

citation: Perez-Salinas et al., Data Re-uploading for a Universal Quantum Classifier
mission_and_task: Synthetic single- and multi-qubit classification
objective_and_constraints: Classify complex low-dimensional datasets

citation: Caro et al., Encoding-Dependent Generalization Bounds for Parametrized Quantum Circuits
